fact: September 10 is exactly 253 days into the year, which means there are only 112 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime spider season - house spiders are frantically searching for mates and that's why you suddenly see them everywhere indoors!
The sun is setting about 2-3 minutes earlier each day around September 10, making the days shrink faster than a deflating balloon.
September 10 falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, when people born under this sign are supposedly extra organized and picky about everything.
Monarch butterflies are typically in full migration mode around this date, with some flying over 2,000 miles to Mexico - that's like flying from New York to Denver!
In many places, morning dew starts getting heavier around this date because the air can't hold as much moisture when it gets cooler.
September 10 is roughly 11 days before the autumn equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal everywhere on Earth.
Squirrels go absolutely nuts around this time, burying up to 10,000 acorns each to prepare for winter - and they forget where half of them are!
The moon phase on September 10 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you get some of the best stargazing of early fall.
In the Southern Hemisphere, September 10 marks the heart of spring, when jacaranda trees often explode with purple flowers that carpet entire streets.
Apple trees typically hit peak ripeness around this date in many northern regions, making it perfect timing for apple picking season.
September 10 sits in the 37th week of the year, which sounds totally random but means we're about 71% done with the entire year!
